description_long: This is a rectangular stone obelisk embedded in a rough, tan-colored wall, featuring a prominent white plaque with an inscription on its front face. The plaque is bordered by dark metal fasteners at the corners and shows some weathering and chipping at the bottom edge. The obelisk's surface is textured and uneven, consistent with aged masonry, and the plaque appears to be made of marble or a similar polished stone. The inscription is in a serifed, classical style, though some characters are worn and partially illegible. The sides of the obelisk are thin and reveal the depth of the plaque relative to the wall. There are no visible decorative motifs or additional structures surrounding the obelisk.
description: A rectangular stone obelisk set in a rough wall, featuring a white inscribed plaque with metal fasteners and visible weathering.
description_view_0: Front view showing the inscribed white plaque, metal fasteners, and chipped lower edge against a rough stone wall.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the thinness of the obelisk and the depth of the plaque relative to the wall.
description_view_2: Rear view with the inscription visible in reverse, showing the same plaque and wall texture as the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again highlighting the thinness and embedded nature of the obelisk.
attribute_inscription: A rectangular white plaque on the front face bears an inscription in a serifed, classical style, possibly Latin. The text is partially legible and appears weathered, with some characters faded or chipped.
attribute_base_design: The base is integrated into the wall, flat and rectangular, with no visible steps or decorative features. The lower edge of the plaque is chipped and slightly uneven.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is not distinctly visible; the obelisk is rectangular and terminates flush with the wall surface.
